refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR uses 'support achieving' which can be interpreted as including implementation details or methods. Replacing 'support achieving' with 'enable achieving' keeps the focus on the functional outcome without implying how it is done, maintaining solution neutrality. The FR contains a single obligation focused on energy efficiency target achievement, so it is atomic.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the energy_per_ton metric (energy_used divided by ton_moved) over the agreed evaluation period and verify it meets or is less than the customer-defined energy efficiency target (E_target).
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined energy efficiency target.

candidate_dps: Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control
Optimize operational parameters dynamically to minimize energy per ton
Incorporate energy recovery and reuse mechanisms
Design system components for high energy efficiency by default
Enable adaptive load balancing to reduce energy waste
selected_dp.selected_dp: Optimize operational parameters dynamically to minimize energy per ton

potential_sources: Dependency between energy monitoring and operational optimization modules
Interaction between load balancing and energy recovery systems
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on dynamic optimization, which can be modularized separately from monitoring and recovery functions, supporting independence if interfaces are well defined.

assumptions_risks: Accurate and timely measurement of energy and tonnage is available
Operational policies allow dynamic parameter adjustments
Energy efficiency improvements do not compromise safety or other critical requirements
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including a DP for energy consumption monitoring to ensure measurement accuracy and support optimization feedback loops.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for managing and optimizing operational parameters of the system to minimize energy consumption per ton.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational data and sensor inputs necessary for optimization.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends optimized operational commands to vehicle or other subsystems.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communicates with external systems for data exchange and updates.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as optimization logic fits well within NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion as most data and control remain internal.
Partial containment possible but optimization logic would be spread across vehicle control systems, increasing diffusion.
Low containment; teleoperation focuses on remote control, not optimization logic.
Very low containment; safety services unrelated to optimization of operational parameters.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the DP of optimizing operational parameters dynamically, as it centralizes the optimization logic and minimizes diffusion across other modules.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on real-time telemetry data availability and quality.
Risk of increased coupling if optimization commands affect multiple vehicle subsystems without clear interface boundaries.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Optimize operational parameters dynamically to minimize energy per ton' directly addresses the FR of enabling achievement of the customer-defined energy efficiency target. The DP logically satisfies the FR by focusing on minimizing energy per ton, which aligns with the acceptance criteria of meeting or being below the energy target.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on real-time telemetry data availability and quality may cause coupling issues.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating that the DP is well contained within a single IM boundary. Other modules show lower containment and higher diffusion, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation module. The optimization logic is centralized, minimizing diffusion risk.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through measurement of energy_per_ton against the target over the evaluation period, as specified in the acceptance criteria. The NGHE Solution module interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, Network Link) support data acquisition and command issuance necessary for verification.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and appropriate, with well-defined interfaces and ownership boundaries that support realization and verification of the FR.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly define interface contracts to ensure telemetry data quality and availability to mitigate dependency risks.
Establish clear interface boundaries with vehicle subsystems to reduce coupling when sending optimization commands.
Include monitoring and fallback mechanisms within NGHE Solution to handle telemetry data issues and maintain safe operation per worksite safety plan.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current functional requirement is adequately addressed by the design parameter and implementation module. The DP logically satisfies the FR, the IM containment and diffusion risks are low and well managed, and verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed. Although there are some recommended revisions to interface contracts and monitoring mechanisms to mitigate dependency risks, these do not necessitate further decomposition at this stage. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to finalize this level before proceeding further.

revise.why: To explicitly define interface contracts ensuring telemetry data quality and availability, establish clear interface boundaries with vehicle subsystems to reduce coupling, and include monitoring and fallback mechanisms within the NGHE Solution module to handle telemetry data issues and maintain safe operation per the worksite safety plan.

assumptions: Telemetry data availability and quality can be ensured through interface contracts.
The NGHE Solution module can incorporate monitoring and fallback mechanisms without requiring further decomposition.
Verification of energy_per_ton against target is sufficient to confirm FR satisfaction.
open_risks: Dependency on real-time telemetry data availability and quality may cause coupling issues if not properly managed.
Potential operational risks if fallback mechanisms are insufficient during telemetry data failures.
evidence_needed: Demonstration of interface contracts enforcing telemetry data quality and availability.
Validation of monitoring and fallback mechanisms effectiveness in NGHE Solution module.
Verification test results showing energy_per_ton meets or is below target under operational conditions.
